results.name: CLAUDE.md project instructions
results.fix: Create CLAUDE.md with project-specific instructions, build commands, and coding conventions.

results.name: Mermaid architecture diagram
results.fix: Add a Mermaid diagram to CLAUDE.md showing project architecture. Saves 73% tokens vs prose.

results.name: Path-specific rules
results.fix: Add rules for different file types (frontend vs backend conventions).

results.name: CLAUDE.md uses @path imports for modularity
results.fix: Use @path syntax in CLAUDE.md to split instructions into focused modules (e.g. @docs/coding-style.md). You can also use .claude/rules/ for path-specific rules.

results.name: CLAUDE.md under 200 lines (concise)
results.fix: Keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines. Use @import or .claude/rules/ to split large instructions.

results.name: Verification criteria in CLAUDE.md
results.fix: Add test/lint/build commands to CLAUDE.md so Claude can verify its own work.

results.name: CLAUDE.md contains a test command
results.fix: Add an explicit test command to CLAUDE.md (e.g. "Run `npm test` before committing").

results.name: CLAUDE.md contains a lint command
results.fix: Add a lint command to CLAUDE.md so Claude auto-formats and checks code style.

results.name: CLAUDE.md contains a build command
results.fix: Add a build command to CLAUDE.md so Claude can verify compilation before committing.

results.name: .claude/ tracked in git
results.fix: Remove .claude/ from .gitignore (keep .claude/settings.local.json ignored).

results.name: .gitignore blocks .env files
results.fix: Add .env to .gitignore to prevent leaking secrets.

results.name: .gitignore blocks node_modules
results.fix: Add node_modules/ to .gitignore.

results.name: CLAUDE.md has no embedded API keys
results.fix: Remove API keys from CLAUDE.md. Use environment variables or .env files instead.

results.name: Custom slash commands
results.fix: Create custom commands for repeated workflows (/test, /deploy, /review).

results.name: 3+ slash commands for rich workflow
results.fix: Add at least 3 slash commands to cover your main workflows (test, deploy, review, etc.).

results.name: Has /deploy or /release command
results.fix: Create a /deploy or /release command for one-click deployments.

results.name: Has /review command
results.fix: Create a /review command for code review workflows.

results.name: Custom skills
results.fix: Create skills at .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description). Each skill is a directory with a SKILL.md file.

results.name: 2+ skills for specialization
results.fix: Add at least 2 skills covering different workflows (e.g. code-review, test-writer).

results.name: Custom agents
results.fix: Create specialized agents (security-reviewer, test-writer) in .claude/agents/.

results.name: 2+ agents for delegation
results.fix: Add at least 2 agents for specialized tasks (e.g. security-reviewer, test-writer).

results.name: 2+ rules files for granular control
results.fix: Add path-specific rules for different parts of the codebase (frontend, backend, tests).

results.name: Permission configuration
results.fix: Configure allow/deny permission lists for safe tool usage.

results.name: Deny rules configured in permissions
results.fix: Add permissions.deny rules to block dangerous operations (e.g. rm -rf, dropping databases).

results.name: Default mode is not bypassPermissions
results.fix: Do not set defaultMode to bypassPermissions. Use explicit allow rules instead.

results.name: Secrets protection configured
results.fix: Add permissions.deny rules to block reading .env files and secrets directories.

results.name: Security review command awareness
results.fix: Add /security-review to your workflow. Claude Code has built-in OWASP Top 10 scanning.

results.name: Hooks for automation
results.fix: Add hooks in .claude/settings.json under the "hooks" key. Supported events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Notification, Stop, StopFailure, SubagentStop, and more.

results.name: Hooks configured in settings
results.fix: Add hooks in .claude/settings.json for automated enforcement (lint-on-save, test-on-commit).

results.name: PreToolUse hook configured
results.fix: Add PreToolUse hooks for validation before tool calls (e.g. block writes to protected files).

results.name: PostToolUse hook configured
results.fix: Add PostToolUse hooks for auto-lint or auto-format after file writes.

results.name: SessionStart hook configured
results.fix: Add a SessionStart hook for initialization tasks (log rotation, state loading, etc.).

results.name: Frontend design skill for anti-AI-slop
results.fix: Install the official frontend-design skill for better UI output quality.

results.name: Tailwind CSS configured
results.fix: Consider adding Tailwind CSS for rapid, consistent UI styling with Claude.

results.name: Has Dockerfile
results.fix: Add a Dockerfile for containerized builds and deployments.

results.name: Has docker-compose.yml
results.fix: Add docker-compose.yml for multi-service local development.

results.name: CI pipeline configured
results.fix: Add a CI pipeline (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI, etc.) for automated testing and deployment.

results.name: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)
results.fix: Add Terraform files for infrastructure-as-code management.

results.name: Has README.md
results.fix: Add a README.md with project overview, setup instructions, and usage.

results.name: Has CHANGELOG.md
results.fix: Add a CHANGELOG.md to track notable changes across versions.

results.name: Has CONTRIBUTING.md
results.fix: Add a CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines and code standards.

results.name: Has LICENSE file
results.fix: Add a LICENSE file to clarify usage rights.

results.name: Has .editorconfig
results.fix: Add .editorconfig for consistent formatting across editors and Claude.

results.name: Node version pinned
results.fix: Add .nvmrc, .node-version, or engines.node in package.json to pin Node version.

results.name: CLAUDE.md mentions /compact or compaction
results.fix: Add compaction guidance to CLAUDE.md (e.g. "Run /compact when context is heavy").

results.name: Context management awareness
results.fix: Add context management tips to CLAUDE.md to help Claude stay within token limits.

results.name: MCP servers configured
results.fix: Configure MCP servers in .mcp.json at project root. Use `claude mcp add` to add servers. Project-level MCP is committed to git for team sharing.

results.name: 2+ MCP servers for rich tooling
results.fix: Add at least 2 MCP servers for broader tool coverage (e.g. database + search).

results.name: Context7 MCP for real-time docs
results.fix: Add Context7 MCP server for real-time documentation lookup (always up-to-date library docs).

results.name: XML tags for structured prompts
results.fix: Add clear rules sections to CLAUDE.md. XML tags (<constraints>) are optional but improve clarity.

results.name: CLAUDE.md contains code examples
results.fix: Add code examples (few-shot) in CLAUDE.md to show preferred patterns and conventions.

results.name: CLAUDE.md defines a role or persona
results.fix: Define a role or persona in CLAUDE.md (e.g. "You are a senior backend engineer...").

results.name: XML constraint blocks in CLAUDE.md
results.fix: Wrap critical rules in <constraints> XML blocks for 40% better adherence.

results.name: Claude Code Channels awareness
results.fix: Claude Code Channels (v2.1.80+) bridges Telegram/Discord/iMessage to your session.

results.name: CLAUDE.md mentions current Claude features
results.fix: Your CLAUDE.md may be outdated. Modern Claude Code supports hooks, skills, agents, MCP, worktrees, and extended thinking. Mention the ones you use.

results.name: CLAUDE.local.md for personal overrides
results.fix: Create CLAUDE.local.md for personal preferences that should not be committed (add to .gitignore).

results.name: CLAUDE.md has no obvious contradictions
results.fix: CLAUDE.md may contain contradictory instructions. Review for conflicting rules (e.g., "always X" and "never X" about the same topic).

results.name: Hooks use specific matchers (not catch-all)
results.fix: Hooks without matchers run on every tool call. Use matchers like "Write|Edit" or "Bash" to target specific tools.

results.name: Commands use $ARGUMENTS for flexibility
results.fix: Commands without $ARGUMENTS are static. Use $ARGUMENTS to make them flexible: "Fix the issue: $ARGUMENTS"

results.name: Subagents have max-turns limit
results.fix: Subagents without max-turns can run indefinitely. Add "max-turns: 50" to subagent YAML frontmatter.

results.name: /security-review command or workflow
results.fix: Claude Code has built-in /security-review (OWASP Top 10). Add it to your workflow or create a /security command.

results.name: Test coverage or strategy mentioned
results.fix: Mention your testing strategy in CLAUDE.md (unit, integration, E2E, coverage targets).

results.name: At least one subagent restricts tools
results.fix: Add allowed-tools to subagent frontmatter (e.g. allowed-tools: Read Grep Bash) for safer delegation.

results.name: Auto-memory or memory management mentioned
results.fix: Claude Code supports auto-memory for cross-session learning. Mention your memory strategy if relevant.

results.name: Sandbox or isolation mentioned
results.fix: Claude Code supports sandboxed command execution. Consider enabling it for untrusted operations.

results.name: Deny rules cover 3+ patterns
results.fix: Add at least 3 deny rules: rm -rf, force-push, and .env reads. More patterns = safer Claude.

results.name: Git attribution configured
results.fix: Decide on git attribution: set attribution.commit or includeCoAuthoredBy in settings.

results.name: Effort level or thinking configuration
results.fix: Configure effortLevel or mention thinking strategy in CLAUDE.md for task-appropriate reasoning depth.

results.name: Audit snapshot history exists
results.fix: Run `npx nerviq --snapshot` to start tracking your setup score over time.

results.name: Worktree or parallel sessions mentioned
results.fix: Claude Code supports git worktrees for parallel isolated sessions. Mention if relevant.

results.name: CLAUDE.md includes "do not" instructions
results.fix: Add explicit "do not" rules to CLAUDE.md. Negative constraints reduce common mistakes.

results.name: CLAUDE.md includes output or style guidance
results.fix: Add coding style and naming conventions to CLAUDE.md so Claude matches your project patterns.

results.name: GitHub Actions or CI configured
results.fix: Add CI pipeline for automated testing. Claude Code has a GitHub Action for audit gates.

results.name: CLAUDE.md describes what the project does
results.fix: Start CLAUDE.md with a clear project description. Claude needs to know what your project does.

results.name: CLAUDE.md documents directory structure
results.fix: Document your directory structure in CLAUDE.md so Claude navigates your codebase efficiently.

results.name: 2+ hook event types configured
results.fix: Add at least 2 hook types (e.g. PostToolUse for linting + SessionStart for initialization).

results.name: StopFailure hook for error tracking
results.fix: Add a StopFailure hook to log API errors and unexpected stops. Note: StopFailure (errors) is different from Stop (normal completion).

results.name: At least one skill uses paths for scoping
results.fix: Add paths to skill frontmatter to scope when skills activate (e.g. paths: ["src/**/*.ts"]).

results.name: MCP servers have environment configuration
results.fix: Configure environment variables for MCP servers that need authentication (e.g. GITHUB_TOKEN).

results.name: .gitignore excludes settings.local.json
results.fix: Add .claude/settings.local.json to .gitignore. Personal overrides should not be committed.

results.name: .env.example or .env.template exists
results.fix: Add .env.example so new developers know which environment variables are needed.

results.name: package.json has dev/test/build scripts
results.fix: Add scripts to package.json (test, dev, build). Claude uses these for verification.

results.name: Type checking configured (TypeScript or similar)
results.fix: Add type checking configuration. Type-safe code produces fewer Claude errors.

results.name: No deprecated patterns detected
results.fix: CLAUDE.md references deprecated API patterns (human_prompt/assistant_prompt). Update to current Messages API conventions.

results.name: CLAUDE.md has substantive content
results.fix: CLAUDE.md exists but lacks substance. Add at least 2 sections (## headings) and include your test/build/lint commands.

results.name: Project-level .mcp.json exists
results.fix: Create .mcp.json at project root for team-shared MCP servers. Use `claude mcp add --project` to add servers.

results.name: Notification hook for alerts
results.fix: Add a Notification hook to capture alerts and status updates from Claude during long tasks.

results.name: SubagentStop hook for delegation tracking
results.fix: Add a SubagentStop hook to track when delegated subagent tasks complete.

results.name: Path-specific rules in .claude/rules/
results.fix: Create .claude/rules/ with path-specific rules for different parts of your codebase (e.g. frontend.md, backend.md).

results.name: CLAUDE.local.md in .gitignore
results.fix: Add CLAUDE.local.md to .gitignore — it contains personal overrides that should not be committed.

results.name: pyproject.toml exists for Python packaging
results.fix: Add pyproject.toml to declare modern Python packaging, tooling, and metadata.

results.name: Type hints used in Python code
results.fix: Add type hints in main Python modules or configure mypy/pyright with py.typed support.

results.name: Python linter configured
results.fix: Configure a Python linter such as ruff, flake8, or pylint in pyproject.toml or a dedicated config file.

results.name: Python formatter configured
results.fix: Configure formatting with black, ruff format, isort, or a Prettier override that explicitly covers Python files.

results.name: Python test framework present
results.fix: Add pytest.ini, conftest.py, tox.ini, or pyproject.toml pytest configuration so the test framework is explicit.

results.name: Virtual environment directories ignored in git
results.fix: Ignore `.venv/`, `venv/`, or `env/` in .gitignore so local environments do not get committed.

results.name: Requirements files use pinned versions
results.fix: Pin Python requirements with `==` or direct references so installs stay reproducible.

results.name: Python security scanner configured
results.fix: Configure bandit, safety, or pip-audit in dependencies, pre-commit, or CI.

results.name: pre-commit configured with Python hooks
results.fix: Add `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with Python-focused hooks such as ruff, black, mypy, or bandit.

results.name: Docstrings present in main Python files
results.fix: Add module, class, or function docstrings in the main Python source files.

results.name: CI runs Python tests
results.fix: Run Python tests in CI with pytest, unittest, tox, or nox.

results.name: Python coverage configured
results.fix: Configure coverage.py or pytest-cov in project config or CI.

results.name: Modern Python package manager lockfile present
results.fix: Commit a Poetry, PDM, uv, or Pipenv lockfile for reproducible dependency resolution.

results.name: Minimum Python version specified
results.fix: Specify the supported Python version with `.python-version`, `requires-python`, or `python_requires`.

results.name: Async Python patterns used
results.fix: Adopt explicit async patterns such as asyncio, aiohttp, FastAPI, or `async def` where concurrent workflows matter.

results.name: Python project includes an environment example file
results.fix: Add `.env.example` or `.env.sample` so required Python environment variables are documented.

results.name: Python database migration tooling present
results.fix: Use Alembic or Django migrations and keep the migration surface committed in the repo.

results.name: Python structured logging configured
results.fix: Configure logging with Python logging config, structlog, or loguru for consistent operational signals.

results.name: Python API schema or model definitions present
results.fix: Define API schemas with OpenAPI, Pydantic, Marshmallow, or typed request/response models.

results.name: Python container image uses a Python base
results.fix: Use an official Python image such as `python:3.12-slim` when containerizing Python services.

results.name: Python dev and test dependency groups separated
results.fix: Separate Python dev and test dependencies with Poetry groups, optional-dependencies, or extras_require.

results.name: Python tool path configuration present
results.fix: Add `pyrightconfig.json` or VS Code Python settings so tooling resolves imports and environments consistently.

results.name: Python monorepo-friendly package layout present
results.fix: Use a `src/` layout or namespace package configuration for larger multi-package Python repos.

results.name: Custom Python exception classes defined
results.fix: Define custom exception classes for domain-specific Python error handling instead of only raising generic exceptions.

results.name: Python data validation library used
results.fix: Use Pydantic, Marshmallow, attrs, or similar validation libraries for structured Python inputs and models.

results.name: go.mod exists for Go module management
results.fix: Initialize the repository as a Go module with `go mod init` and commit `go.mod`.

results.name: Go linter configured
results.fix: Configure golangci-lint in the repo or CI for consistent Go lint enforcement.

results.name: Go test files present
results.fix: Add `_test.go` files so Go packages have executable unit or integration tests.

results.name: go vet runs in automation
results.fix: Run `go vet` in CI or the project Makefile to catch common Go mistakes.

results.name: gofmt or goimports enforced
results.fix: Run `gofmt` or `goimports` in CI, pre-commit, or developer tooling.

results.name: go mod tidy runs in automation
results.fix: Run `go mod tidy` in CI or the Makefile so module metadata stays clean.

results.name: Go build tags or constraints used
results.fix: Use `//go:build` constraints when a Go package depends on build tags or platform-specific variants.

results.name: Go errors use wrapping patterns
results.fix: Wrap Go errors with `fmt.Errorf(... %w ...)` or similar patterns to preserve context.

results.name: Go interfaces stay small
results.fix: Keep Go interfaces small and focused; split interfaces that define more than five methods.

results.name: Go services use context.Context
results.fix: Pass `context.Context` through handlers and services so cancellation and deadlines are propagated correctly.

results.name: Exported Go structs include tags
results.fix: Add struct tags such as `json`, `yaml`, or `db` on exported Go types that cross boundaries.

results.name: Go Makefile includes build, test, and lint targets
results.fix: Add a Makefile with `build`, `test`, and `lint` targets for common Go workflows.

results.name: Exported Go functions have doc comments
results.fix: Add Go doc comments above exported functions so package APIs remain self-describing.

results.name: Go security scanner configured
results.fix: Configure `gosec` or `staticcheck` in CI or the Makefile for Go security and static analysis checks.

results.name: CI runs Go tests
results.fix: Run `go test ./...` in CI so Go packages are verified on every change.

results.name: Go Dockerfile uses multi-stage build
results.fix: Use a multi-stage Go Dockerfile: compile in a `golang` image and run from a minimal final image.

results.name: Go coverage reporting configured
results.fix: Add `go test -cover` to CI or developer commands so Go coverage is tracked explicitly.

results.name: Go HTTP framework detected
results.fix: Use a well-supported Go HTTP framework such as Gin, Echo, Fiber, or Chi when building API services.

results.name: Go database migration tooling present
results.fix: Add a Go migration tool such as golang-migrate, goose, or Atlas and keep migration files in the repo.

results.name: Go dependency injection pattern present
results.fix: Use Wire, Fx, Dig, or an equivalent composition pattern when Go dependency graphs become complex.

results.name: Cargo.toml exists
results.fix: Add a `Cargo.toml` manifest so Rust dependencies, metadata, and build settings are tracked explicitly.

results.name: Rust edition specified in Cargo.toml
results.fix: Specify a Rust edition such as `edition = "2021"` in `Cargo.toml` so tooling and language semantics are pinned.

results.name: Clippy configured
results.fix: Configure `cargo clippy` in CI, pre-commit, or `.cargo/config.toml` so linting is enforced consistently.

results.name: rustfmt configured
results.fix: Add `rustfmt.toml` or `.rustfmt.toml` to capture Rust formatting expectations in version control.

results.name: Rust tests exist
results.fix: Add Rust unit or integration tests using `#[test]` functions or a `tests/` directory.

results.name: Rust benchmarks present
results.fix: Add Rust benchmarks through `benches/`, `criterion`, or benchmark annotations when performance-sensitive code matters.

results.name: CI runs cargo test
results.fix: Run `cargo test` in CI so Rust correctness is verified automatically on every change.

results.name: Cargo.lock handling is appropriate
results.fix: Commit `Cargo.lock` for binaries, or explicitly ignore it for library-only crates when that is your chosen policy.

results.name: Unsafe blocks are documented
results.fix: Document each `unsafe` block with a nearby `SAFETY:` comment explaining the invariants being upheld.

results.name: Rust error handling strategy present
results.fix: Use `thiserror`, `anyhow`, or explicit error types so Rust errors remain structured and descriptive.

results.name: Rust async runtime configured
results.fix: Declare an async runtime such as Tokio or async-std when the Rust project uses asynchronous workflows.

results.name: Serde serialization configured
results.fix: Add `serde` and related crates when Rust data crosses process, storage, or network boundaries.

results.name: Public Rust items have doc comments
results.fix: Add `///` doc comments above public Rust APIs so crates are easier to consume and maintain.

results.name: Rust security audit tooling configured
results.fix: Configure `cargo-audit` or `cargo-deny` in CI or project automation to scan Rust dependencies for risk.

results.name: Minimum supported Rust version specified
results.fix: Set `rust-version` in `Cargo.toml` so the project’s MSRV is explicit for contributors and CI.

results.name: Cargo workspace configured for multi-crate projects
results.fix: Add a root Cargo workspace when the Rust repository contains multiple crates.

results.name: Rust build script present when needed
results.fix: Use `build.rs` when the project needs generated bindings, codegen, or compile-time environment setup.

results.name: Rust feature flags defined
results.fix: Define Cargo feature flags when Rust functionality needs optional capabilities or slimmed dependency sets.

results.name: Rust cross-compilation targets configured
results.fix: Configure Rust cross-compilation targets in CI or `.cargo/config.toml` when builds must run across architectures or platforms.

results.name: Rust Dockerfile present
results.fix: Use a Dockerfile that references Rust or Cargo when the project’s build and release flow is containerized.

results.name: Maven or Gradle build file exists
results.fix: Add `pom.xml`, `build.gradle`, or `build.gradle.kts` so the Java build is defined in version control.

results.name: Java version specified
results.fix: Specify the Java version in Maven or Gradle so compilation and runtime expectations stay explicit.

results.name: Spring Boot detected
results.fix: Use Spring Boot dependencies when the Java service relies on Spring auto-configuration and conventions.

results.name: Java test framework configured
results.fix: Add JUnit, TestNG, or Spring Boot test dependencies so Java tests have a standard runner.

results.name: Java linter configured
results.fix: Configure Checkstyle, SpotBugs, or PMD so Java code quality rules run consistently.

results.name: Java formatter configured
results.fix: Configure Spotless, google-java-format, or an `.editorconfig` so Java formatting stays consistent.

results.name: CI runs Java tests
results.fix: Run `mvn test`, `mvnw test`, `gradle test`, or `gradlew test` in CI so Java changes are validated automatically.

results.name: Java security scanner configured
results.fix: Configure OWASP Dependency-Check, Snyk, or SpotBugs security rules for Java dependency and code scanning.

results.name: Java documentation configured
results.fix: Generate Javadocs or add doc comments on public Java types so the API remains understandable to contributors.

results.name: Java profiles or build variants configured
results.fix: Use Maven profiles, Spring profiles, or Gradle build variants when Java environments need explicit separation.

results.name: Java Dockerfile references Java build/runtime
results.fix: Use a Dockerfile or build image that references Java, Maven, or Gradle when the application is containerized.

results.name: Java API framework detected
results.fix: Use Spring MVC, JAX-RS, or Micronaut conventions explicitly when the Java project exposes an API.

results.name: Java database migration tooling present
results.fix: Add Flyway, Liquibase, or repo-managed migration files so Java schema changes are repeatable and reviewable.

results.name: Java message queue integration detected
results.fix: Use explicit Kafka, RabbitMQ, or JMS integrations when the Java service relies on asynchronous messaging.

results.name: Java caching configured
results.fix: Configure Redis, Ehcache, Caffeine, or Spring Cache when Java services benefit from explicit caching layers.

results.name: Java monitoring dependencies detected
results.fix: Add Actuator, Micrometer, or Prometheus integrations so Java services expose health and metrics data.

results.name: Java logging configured
results.fix: Use SLF4J, Logback, or Log4j so Java application logging is explicit and configurable.

results.name: Java multi-module structure configured
results.fix: Configure a root Maven or Gradle multi-module definition when the Java repository contains multiple modules.

results.name: Java dependency injection pattern present
results.fix: Use Spring DI, Guice, or Dagger patterns so Java object graphs stay explicit and testable.

results.name: Java application property files exist
results.fix: Add `application.properties` or `application.yml` when the Java service relies on conventional runtime configuration files.

results.name: Gemfile exists

results.name: PHPStan/Psalm configured (phpstan.neon)
results.fix: Configure PHPStan (phpstan.neon) or Psalm (psalm.xml) for static analysis.

results.name: PHP CS Fixer configured (.php-cs-fixer.php)
results.fix: Add .php-cs-fixer.php for consistent code formatting.

results.name: PHPUnit configured (phpunit.xml)
results.fix: Configure PHPUnit with phpunit.xml for testing.

results.name: Laravel .env.example exists
results.fix: Create .env.example with all required environment variables documented.

results.name: Laravel APP_KEY not committed
results.fix: Ensure .env with APP_KEY is in .gitignore — never commit application keys.

results.name: Laravel migrations exist (database/migrations/)
results.fix: Create database migrations in database/migrations/ directory.

results.name: Artisan commands documented
results.fix: Document key Artisan commands (migrate, seed, make:*) in project instructions.

results.name: Queue worker documented
results.fix: Document queue worker setup (php artisan queue:work, Horizon).

results.name: Laravel Pint configured (pint.json)
results.fix: Configure Laravel Pint (pint.json) for code style enforcement.

results.name: Vite/Mix asset bundling documented
results.fix: Document asset bundling setup (Vite or Mix) in project instructions.

results.name: Laravel config caching documented
results.fix: Document config/route caching strategy (php artisan config:cache) for production.

results.name: Composer scripts defined
results.fix: Define composer scripts for common tasks (test, lint, analyze) in composer.json.

results.name: Has /llms.txt or llms.txt for LLM context
results.fix: Add llms.txt to provide LLM-friendly project context. See llmstxt.org for the standard.

results.name: MCP budget not over-provisioned (≤10 servers, ≤80 tools)
results.fix: Too many MCP servers (>10) degrades performance. Remove unused servers or consolidate.

results.name: Hook scripts handle exit codes correctly
results.fix: Hooks should use explicit exit codes: 0=success, 1=warning, 2=block. See Claude Code docs.

results.name: Loop safety boundaries configured
results.fix: Document loop safety limits such as maxTurns, maxTokens, or iteration caps in CLAUDE.md, settings, or hook guards.

results.name: Consistency/pass@k evaluation mentioned
results.fix: Mention pass@k or consistency testing in CLAUDE.md or test configuration so repeated-run quality evaluation is explicit.

results.name: Instinct-to-skill progression documented
results.fix: Document a progressive learning path that turns repeated instincts into reusable skills or phased practices.

results.name: pubspec.yaml exists
results.fix: Add a `pubspec.yaml` manifest so Flutter/Dart dependencies and project metadata are tracked.

results.name: Flutter analysis options configured
results.fix: Add analysis_options.yaml for Dart/Flutter linting rules.

results.name: Flutter tests exist
results.fix: Add Flutter widget or unit tests in a `test/` directory with `_test.dart` suffix.

results.name: Flutter lint package configured
results.fix: Add `flutter_lints` or `very_good_analysis` to pubspec.yaml dev_dependencies for consistent linting.

results.name: Flutter platform directories present
results.fix: Run `flutter create .` to generate `android/` and `ios/` platform directories.

results.name: Flutter web support enabled
results.fix: Run `flutter create --platforms=web .` to add web support.

results.name: Flutter localization configured
results.fix: Add `l10n.yaml` or the `intl` package to support localization and internationalization.

results.name: Flutter state management configured
results.fix: Add a state management solution such as `riverpod`, `bloc`, or `provider` to pubspec.yaml.

results.name: Flutter routing configured
results.fix: Add `go_router` or `auto_route` for declarative, type-safe Flutter routing.

results.name: CI runs flutter test
results.fix: Add `flutter test` to your CI workflow so tests run automatically on every change.

results.name: Flutter code generation configured
results.fix: Add `build_runner` and/or `freezed` to pubspec.yaml for code generation support.

results.name: Flutter Firebase integration
results.fix: Add Firebase packages to pubspec.yaml and run `flutterfire configure` to generate firebase_options.dart.

results.name: Flutter assets configured
results.fix: Add an `assets:` section in pubspec.yaml to declare images, fonts, and other bundled resources.

results.name: Flutter flavors configured
results.fix: Configure Flutter flavors for environment-specific builds (dev, staging, production).

results.name: Flutter Dockerfile present
results.fix: Add a Dockerfile that includes the Flutter or Dart SDK for containerized builds.

results.name: Swift package or Xcode project exists
results.fix: Add a `Package.swift` or `.xcodeproj` to define your Swift project structure.

results.name: SwiftLint configured
results.fix: Add `.swiftlint.yml` to enforce Swift coding conventions.

results.name: Swift tests exist
results.fix: Add Swift tests in a `Tests/` directory using XCTest.

results.name: SwiftFormat configured
results.fix: Add `.swiftformat` to enforce consistent Swift formatting.

results.name: CI runs swift test or xcodebuild
results.fix: Add `swift test` or `xcodebuild test` to your CI workflow.

results.name: Swift doc comments present
results.fix: Add `///` documentation comments to public Swift APIs.

results.name: Swift Package Manager used
results.fix: Add `Package.swift` to use Swift Package Manager for dependency management.

results.name: Swift tools version specified
results.fix: Add `// swift-tools-version:5.9` (or appropriate version) at the top of Package.swift.

results.name: Swift access control used
results.fix: Use `public`/`internal` access control in Swift files to define clear API boundaries.

results.name: Swift concurrency used
results.fix: Adopt Swift structured concurrency with `async`/`await` for modern asynchronous code.

results.name: Kotlin Gradle plugin configured
results.fix: Apply the Kotlin Gradle plugin in build.gradle.kts to enable Kotlin compilation.

results.name: Kotlin version specified
results.fix: Pin the Kotlin version in gradle.properties or build.gradle.kts for reproducible builds.

results.name: Kotlin linter configured
results.fix: Add `ktlint` or `detekt` to enforce Kotlin code style and static analysis.

results.name: Kotlin tests exist
results.fix: Add Kotlin tests in `src/test/` using JUnit or KotlinTest.

results.name: Kotlin Coroutines in dependencies
results.fix: Add `kotlinx-coroutines-core` to dependencies for structured concurrency.

results.name: Kotlin serialization configured
results.fix: Add `kotlinx.serialization` for type-safe, multiplatform serialization.

results.name: Jetpack Compose configured
results.fix: Enable Jetpack Compose in build.gradle.kts for modern declarative Android UI.

results.name: CI runs Kotlin tests
results.fix: Add `./gradlew test` to your CI workflow so Kotlin tests run automatically.

results.name: Kotlin Multiplatform configured
results.fix: Apply the `kotlin-multiplatform` Gradle plugin to share code across JVM, iOS, JS, and Native targets.

results.name: KDoc comments present
results.fix: Add KDoc comments (`/** ... */`) to public Kotlin APIs for documentation generation.

results.name: WebSocket library configured
results.fix: Add a WebSocket library for real-time communication if your app needs live updates.

results.name: Server-Sent Events patterns detected
results.fix: Consider Server-Sent Events (SSE) for server-to-client streaming when full duplex is not needed.

results.name: Real-time database configured
results.fix: Add a real-time database (Firebase, Supabase, Convex) for live data synchronization.

results.name: Pub/sub messaging configured
results.fix: Add a pub/sub messaging system (Redis, NATS, Kafka, RabbitMQ) for decoupled real-time communication.

results.name: WebSocket authentication patterns present
results.fix: Add authentication to WebSocket connections — validate tokens on connect to prevent unauthorized access.

results.name: GraphQL schema defined
results.fix: Define a GraphQL schema using .graphql files or schema-first/code-first approach.

results.name: GraphQL resolvers implemented
results.fix: Implement GraphQL resolvers to handle queries, mutations, and field resolution.

results.name: GraphQL code generation configured
results.fix: Add @graphql-codegen for type-safe GraphQL operations and automatic TypeScript type generation.

results.name: GraphQL N+1 prevention (DataLoader)
results.fix: Use DataLoader or batch loading patterns to prevent N+1 query problems in GraphQL resolvers.

results.name: GraphQL subscriptions configured
results.fix: Configure GraphQL subscriptions with graphql-ws for real-time data pushed to clients.

results.name: OpenTelemetry SDK configured
results.fix: Add OpenTelemetry SDK to your project for unified traces, metrics, and logs collection.

results.name: Prometheus metrics configured
results.fix: Add a Prometheus client library and expose a /metrics endpoint for monitoring.

results.name: Structured logging library
results.fix: Use a structured logging library (winston, pino, structlog, slog, tracing) for machine-readable logs.

results.name: Distributed tracing configured
results.fix: Add a distributed tracing library (Jaeger, Zipkin, OpenTelemetry) for cross-service request tracking.

results.name: Health check endpoint
results.fix: Add a /health or /healthz endpoint for load balancer and orchestrator health checks.

results.name: Alerting system configured
results.fix: Configure alerting (Alertmanager, PagerDuty, OpsGenie) to get notified of production issues.

results.name: Monitoring dashboard defined
results.fix: Add Grafana dashboard JSON files or configure dashboard-as-code for production monitoring.

results.name: Log aggregation configured
results.fix: Configure log aggregation (ELK, Loki, CloudWatch, Datadog) for centralized log analysis.

results.name: Accessibility testing tool
results.fix: Add an accessibility testing tool (axe-core, pa11y, jest-axe) to catch a11y regressions.

results.name: ARIA labels in components
results.fix: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby attributes to interactive components for screen readers.

results.name: WCAG or accessibility in docs
results.fix: Document WCAG compliance level and accessibility standards in your project docs.

results.name: Semantic HTML elements used
results.fix: Use semantic HTML elements (nav, main, article, section, aside) instead of generic div elements.

results.name: Color contrast checking configured
results.fix: Configure a color contrast checking tool (axe, Lighthouse CI, contrast-checker) for WCAG AA compliance.

results.name: Keyboard navigation patterns
results.fix: Implement keyboard navigation with tabindex, key handlers, and focus management for accessible UIs.

results.name: Privacy policy document exists
results.fix: Create a PRIVACY.md or privacy-policy document describing data handling practices.

results.name: Consent management configured
results.fix: Add a consent management solution (CookieConsent, OneTrust, Cookiebot) for GDPR cookie compliance.

results.name: Data retention policy documented
results.fix: Document your data retention policy specifying how long user data is stored and when it is deleted.

results.name: PII handling patterns in code
results.fix: Implement PII handling patterns (redact, anonymize, mask) to protect personal data in logs and storage.

results.name: GDPR/CCPA compliance mentioned
results.fix: Document GDPR/CCPA compliance measures and data protection practices in your project.

results.name: Data encryption in deps or config
results.fix: Use encryption libraries (bcrypt, argon2, crypto) for data at rest and configure TLS for data in transit.

results.name: Error tracking service configured
results.fix: Add an error tracking service (Sentry, Bugsnag, Rollbar, Datadog APM) to catch production errors.

results.name: Error boundaries in frontend
results.fix: Add error boundaries (React ErrorBoundary, Vue errorHandler) to gracefully handle frontend errors.

results.name: Unhandled rejection/exception handler
results.fix: Add handlers for unhandledRejection and uncaughtException to prevent silent failures.

results.name: Error notification/reporting pattern
results.fix: Add error reporting patterns (webhook, Slack alerts, Sentry capture) to get notified of failures.

results.name: SLO/SLA or error budget defined
results.fix: Define SLOs, SLAs, or error budgets in your docs to set clear reliability targets.

results.name: Crash reporting for mobile
results.fix: Add crash reporting (Crashlytics, Sentry Native) to track mobile app crashes in production.

results.name: SBOM file exists
results.fix: Generate an SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) in CycloneDX or SPDX format for supply chain transparency.

results.name: Lock files committed
results.fix: Commit lock files (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Cargo.lock, poetry.lock) for reproducible builds.

results.name: Provenance or sigstore in CI
results.fix: Add npm provenance or sigstore attestation in CI to verify package integrity.

results.name: CI uses frozen lockfile install
results.fix: Use `npm ci` or `--frozen-lockfile` in CI instead of `npm install` for deterministic builds.

results.name: Dependency scanning configured
results.fix: Configure Dependabot, Renovate, or Snyk to automatically scan and update vulnerable dependencies.

results.name: i18n library in dependencies
results.fix: Add an i18n library (i18next, react-intl, vue-i18n, gettext, fluent) for internationalization support.

results.name: Locale files exist
results.fix: Add locale files in a locales/, messages/, or translations/ directory for multi-language support.

results.name: RTL support configured
results.fix: Add RTL (right-to-left) support with dir="rtl" or bidi utilities for languages like Arabic and Hebrew.

results.name: ICU message format or pluralization
results.fix: Use ICU message format or pluralization rules for correct multi-language number/gender handling.

results.name: i18n extraction tool configured
results.fix: Add an i18n extraction tool (i18next-parser, @formatjs/cli) to auto-extract translatable strings.

results.name: Locale-aware date/time formatting
results.fix: Use locale-aware date/time formatting (Intl.DateTimeFormat, date-fns/locale, dayjs/locale) instead of hardcoded formats.

results.name: API versioning pattern present
results.fix: Add API versioning (URL prefix /v1/, header Accept-Version) to manage breaking changes safely.

results.name: Deprecation notices in API code
results.fix: Add @deprecated annotations or Deprecation/Sunset headers to signal API endpoint retirement.

results.name: API changelog exists
results.fix: Add an API changelog (CHANGELOG.md with API section or api-changelog file) to document breaking changes.

results.name: Backward compatibility tests or migrations
results.fix: Add backward compatibility tests or migration scripts to validate API changes don't break clients.

results.name: Versioned API documentation
results.fix: Add versioned API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger spec with version field) for API consumers.

results.name: Cache library in dependencies
results.fix: Add a caching layer (redis, memcached, ioredis, lru-cache) to reduce latency and database load.

results.name: CDN configured
results.fix: Configure a CDN (CloudFront, Cloudflare, Fastly) for static asset delivery and edge caching.

results.name: Cache-Control headers configured
results.fix: Set Cache-Control headers (max-age, s-maxage, stale-while-revalidate) for HTTP response caching.

results.name: Cache invalidation patterns present
results.fix: Implement cache invalidation patterns (purge, bust, invalidate) to prevent serving stale data.

results.name: ETag or Last-Modified caching
results.fix: Implement ETag or Last-Modified headers for conditional HTTP caching and bandwidth savings.

results.name: Rate limiting middleware configured
results.fix: Add rate limiting middleware (express-rate-limit, @nestjs/throttler, rate-limiter-flexible) to protect APIs.

results.name: DDoS protection configured
results.fix: Add DDoS protection (helmet, CORS, WAF, Cloudflare) to defend against abuse and volumetric attacks.

results.name: Retry/backoff strategy in dependencies
results.fix: Add a retry/backoff library (p-retry, tenacity, exponential-backoff) for resilient external calls.

results.name: Request throttling in dependencies
results.fix: Add request throttling (bottleneck, p-throttle) to control outbound API call rates.

results.name: Rate limit headers or 429 responses
results.fix: Return X-RateLimit headers and 429 status codes so clients can handle rate limiting gracefully.

results.name: Feature flag service in dependencies
results.fix: Add a feature flag service (LaunchDarkly, Unleash, Flagsmith, GrowthBook) for safe feature rollouts.

results.name: Feature flag config files exist
results.fix: Add feature flag configuration files (flags.json, features.json, or feature-flags/ directory).

results.name: Feature flag testing present
results.fix: Add tests for feature flag variations to verify behavior under different flag states.

results.name: Flag lifecycle management
results.fix: Add flag lifecycle scripts or docs (flag-audit, remove-flag) to prevent stale flag accumulation.

results.name: Environment-based feature toggles
results.fix: Use environment-based feature toggles (FEATURE_, ENABLE_, FF_ prefixes) for deployment-time configuration.

results.name: README has installation, usage, and contributing sections
results.fix: Ensure README.md includes installation, usage, and contributing sections for developer onboarding.

results.name: CONTRIBUTING.md exists
results.fix: Add CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines, code standards, and PR process.

results.name: API documentation generator configured
results.fix: Add an API documentation generator (typedoc, jsdoc, sphinx, godoc) for auto-generated docs.

results.name: Storybook configured for component docs
results.fix: Add Storybook (.storybook/) for interactive component documentation and visual testing.

results.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md exists
results.fix: Add CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md to set community standards and expectations.

results.name: LICENSE file exists
results.fix: Add a LICENSE file to clarify usage rights and legal terms.

results.name: Monorepo tool configured
results.fix: Configure a monorepo orchestration tool (Turborepo, Nx, Lerna, Rush) for efficient multi-package builds.

results.name: Workspace dependency management configured
results.fix: Configure workspace dependencies (pnpm-workspace.yaml or workspaces in package.json) for cross-package linking.

results.name: Changesets or conventional commits for versioning
results.fix: Add @changesets/cli or conventional commits for coordinated versioning across packages.

results.name: CI uses affected/changed detection
results.fix: Use affected/changed detection in CI (nx affected, turbo --filter) to only build what changed.

results.name: Shared configs across packages
results.fix: Create shared config packages (eslint, tsconfig) referenced across monorepo packages for consistency.

results.name: Lighthouse CI configured
results.fix: Add Lighthouse CI (lighthouserc.js) to enforce performance budgets in your CI pipeline.

results.name: Bundle size check configured
results.fix: Add bundle size checks (size-limit, bundlewatch, @next/bundle-analyzer) to prevent bundle bloat.

results.name: Core Web Vitals tracking configured
results.fix: Add Core Web Vitals tracking (web-vitals, next/web-vitals) for real user performance monitoring.

results.name: Performance regression testing in CI
results.fix: Add performance regression testing in CI (benchmark, lighthouse assert) to catch regressions early.

results.name: Image optimization configured
results.fix: Add image optimization (sharp, imagemin, next/image) to reduce page weight and improve loading.

results.name: Code splitting and lazy loading
results.fix: Use code splitting and lazy loading (React.lazy, dynamic import, loadable) to reduce initial bundle size.

results.name: Testing strategy: test framework detected
results.fix: Document and install a primary test framework (for example Jest, Vitest, Playwright, pytest, or equivalent) so the repo has an explicit testing baseline.

results.name: Testing strategy: coverage configuration exists
results.fix: Add a coverage configuration or script (`coverage`, `nyc`, `c8`, `coverageThreshold`, or equivalent) so test depth is measurable.

results.name: Testing strategy: E2E setup present
results.fix: Add an E2E harness such as Playwright or Cypress, or document why the repo intentionally relies on another end-to-end strategy.

results.name: Testing strategy: snapshot tests mentioned
results.fix: Mention snapshot testing expectations or store snapshots in a conventional location so UI and serializer regressions are reviewable.

results.name: Testing strategy: test command documented
results.fix: Document the canonical test command in repo instructions so contributors and agents can verify changes the same way.

results.name: Testing strategy: CI runs tests
results.fix: Make CI run the project test command so regressions are caught automatically rather than only in local sessions.

results.name: Code quality: linter configured
results.fix: Configure a linter such as ESLint, Ruff, Flake8, or equivalent so code quality rules are enforceable and repeatable.

results.name: Code quality: formatter configured
results.fix: Configure a formatter such as Prettier, Black, Ruff format, rustfmt, or equivalent so style drift does not become manual toil.

results.name: Code quality: dead code detection
results.fix: Add a dead-code scan (`knip`, `ts-prune`, `depcheck`, `vulture`, or equivalent) or document how the repo handles unused code removal.

results.name: Code quality: complexity awareness
results.fix: Document or configure complexity guardrails (for example cyclomatic complexity or small-function guidance) so growth pressure stays visible.

results.name: Code quality: consistent naming documented
results.fix: Document naming conventions such as camelCase, PascalCase, kebab-case, or snake_case so generated and hand-written code stay aligned.

results.name: Code quality: code review process mentioned
results.fix: Mention the code review process in repo guidance or templates so contributors know how changes are checked before merge.

results.name: API design: endpoint documentation present
results.fix: Document the main API endpoints or ship an OpenAPI/Swagger description so integrations stay reviewable.

results.name: API design: versioning mentioned
results.fix: Document the API versioning strategy (`v1`, header-based, or explicit stability policy) so breaking changes are easier to govern.

results.name: API design: error handling patterns defined
results.fix: Document the API error handling shape (for example problem+json, error envelopes, or standard status mapping) so clients get predictable failures.

results.name: API design: rate limiting awareness
results.fix: Mention rate limiting or throttling expectations so public and internal API surfaces have clear abuse boundaries.

results.name: API design: request validation present
results.fix: Use and document request validation (Zod, Joi, class-validator, Pydantic, or equivalent) so invalid inputs fail early and consistently.

results.name: API design: response format consistency described
results.fix: Describe the standard API response shape or schema conventions so consumers know what to expect from every endpoint.

results.name: Database: migration strategy documented
results.fix: Document the migration workflow (`prisma migrate`, `alembic`, SQL migrations, or equivalent) so schema changes are repeatable and reviewable.

results.name: Database: query optimization mentioned
results.fix: Mention query optimization concerns such as indexes, N+1 prevention, pagination, or query plans so performance work has a shared baseline.

results.name: Database: connection pooling addressed
results.fix: Document or configure connection pooling so the database layer does not rely on unbounded per-request connections.

results.name: Database: backup strategy referenced
results.fix: Reference backup and restore expectations so data durability is not left as tribal knowledge.

results.name: Database: schema documentation present
results.fix: Include schema documentation or schema files so contributors can understand the data model without guessing.

results.name: Database: seed data mentioned
results.fix: Document seed data or bootstrap fixtures so contributors can stand up realistic local environments quickly.

results.name: Authentication: auth flow documented
results.fix: Document the authentication flow so login, signup, and trust boundaries are clear for contributors and agents.

results.name: Authentication: token handling guidance
results.fix: Describe how tokens are stored, refreshed, and protected so secrets do not leak into client storage or logs.

results.name: Authentication: session management described
results.fix: Document session lifetime, revocation, and invalidation rules so auth behavior is predictable under change.

results.name: Authentication: RBAC or permissions referenced
results.fix: Reference roles and permission boundaries so the repo has a shared model for authorization checks.

results.name: Authentication: OAuth or SSO mentioned
results.fix: Document OAuth, OIDC, SSO, or SAML expectations if the project uses delegated authentication or enterprise identity.

results.name: Authentication: credential rotation mentioned
results.fix: Mention credential rotation or secret rollover procedures so auth incidents do not require ad-hoc recovery.

results.name: Monitoring: logging configured
results.fix: Configure structured logging or document the logging approach so production diagnostics do not depend on ad-hoc console output.

results.name: Monitoring: error tracking setup
results.fix: Wire error tracking such as Sentry, Bugsnag, or Rollbar, or document the equivalent crash-reporting path.

results.name: Monitoring: APM or metrics mentioned
results.fix: Mention metrics, tracing, or APM so performance and reliability signals are available before incidents escalate.

results.name: Monitoring: health check endpoint referenced
results.fix: Document or implement a health check endpoint (`/health`, `/ready`, `/live`) so uptime checks have a stable target.

results.name: Monitoring: alerting referenced
results.fix: Reference alerting or on-call expectations so incidents have an explicit escalation path.

results.name: Monitoring: log rotation mentioned
results.fix: Mention log retention or rotation so long-running services do not silently fill disks with unbounded logs.

results.name: Dependency management: lockfile present
results.fix: Commit a lockfile so installs stay reproducible across contributors, CI, and agent-driven edits.

results.name: Dependency management: outdated dependency awareness
results.fix: Document or script dependency update checks (`npm outdated`, Renovate, Dependabot, or equivalent) so stale packages are noticed before they become risk.

results.name: Dependency management: license compliance referenced
results.fix: Reference license compliance or OSS review so dependency adoption has an explicit governance path.

results.name: Dependency management: audit command configured
results.fix: Run `npm audit`, `pnpm audit`, `yarn audit`, `pip-audit`, or an equivalent scanner in scripts or CI so supply-chain issues are visible.

results.name: Dependency management: versions pinned deliberately
results.fix: Prefer exact version pins or document why floating ranges are acceptable so dependency drift stays intentional.

results.name: Dependency management: auto-update policy present
results.fix: Add Dependabot, Renovate, or a documented update policy so dependency maintenance does not depend on memory alone.

results.name: Cost optimization: token usage awareness
results.fix: Mention token or context-window costs so AI-heavy workflows do not scale blindly.

results.name: Cost optimization: model selection guidance
results.fix: Document when to use smaller, faster, or cheaper models so the repo does not default every task to the most expensive option.

results.name: Cost optimization: caching to reduce API calls mentioned
results.fix: Mention caching, memoization, or response reuse so repeated requests do not waste tokens or API budget.

results.name: Cost optimization: batch processing mentioned
results.fix: Mention batching or bulk operations where appropriate so repetitive calls can be collapsed into fewer requests.

results.name: Cost optimization: budget guardrails mentioned
results.fix: Document spend or usage guardrails so automation has a visible budget boundary.

results.name: Cost optimization: context pruning guidance
results.fix: Mention context pruning, chunking, or summarization so long prompts do not burn unnecessary tokens.

results.name: Experiment folders contain evidence
results.fix: Add an evidence.md file to each experiment folder documenting results, methodology, and conclusions.
